revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安倍吉平]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0954|death_date={{death date|1027|1|28}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}}}
{{Unreferenced|date=February 2012|qq=}} {{Infobox aristocrat | name = Abe no Yoshihira | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Heian period]] (mid-period) | birth_date = [[Tenryaku]] 8 ([[954]]) | death_date = [[Manju]] 3, [[12th month 18th day (lunar calendar)|18th day of the 12th month]] ([[1027]] [[January 28]]) | alternate_names = | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank|Senior Junior Fourth Rank]]・Assistant Director of the [[Onmyoryo]] | master = [[Emperor Ichijo]]→[[Emperor Sanjo]]→[[Emperor Go-Ichijo]] | clan = [[Abe clan]] | parents = Father: [[Abe no Seimei]] | siblings = Yoshihira, [[Abe no Yoshimasa|Yoshimasa]] | spouse = | children = [[Abe no Tokichika]], [[Abe no Hirakazu]], [[Abe no Kunitoki]], [[Abe no Akichika]], [[Abe no Norichika]] | special_notes = }} '''Abe no Yoshihira''' (安倍 吉平) was a mid-Heian period [[:en:aristocrat|aristocrat]], [[Onmyōji|Onmyoji]], and practitioner of the [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]. He was the eldest son of [[Abe no Seimei]], the {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|qid=Q1047523|lt=Governor of Harima Province|1=Harima Province}} (though some sources suggest otherwise). He held positions such as [[Onmyoryo|Assistant Onmyo Director]], [[Shukeiryo|Assistant Director of the Bureau of Fiscal Affairs]], [[Chief of the Imperial Table Office]], and Chief Administrator of the {{ill|Ministry of Popular Affairs|en|Ministry of Popular Affairs|qid=Q4388365|lt=Ministry of Popular Affairs|1=Ministry of Popular Affairs}}. His court Rank was Senior Junior Fourth Rank. == Career == Yoshihira is known to have served as an Onmyo Director in [[:en:Shoryaku|Shoryaku]] 2 ({{ill|991|en|991|qid=Q24097|lt=991|1=991}}) and as Assistant Director of the Onmyoryo around [[:en:Shoryaku|Shoryaku]] 4 ({{ill|993|en|993|qid=Q24080|lt=993|1=993}}). He was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank in [[:en:Chowa|Chowa]] 5 ({{ill|1016|en|1016|qid=Q19247|lt=1016|1=1016}}). Following the death of his younger brother, [[Abe no Yoshimasa|Yoshimasa]], in {{ill|Kannin|en|Kannin|qid=Q1193951|lt=Kannin|1=Kannin}} 3 ({{ill|1019|en|1019|qid=Q19252|lt=1019|1=1019}}), Yoshihira received an imperial edict regarding the secret [[Tenmonmitsu]] ritual. In [[Jian (Japan)|Jian]] 1 ({{ill|1021|en|1021|qid=Q19257|lt=1021|1=1021}}), he was promoted to Senior Junior Fourth Rank for his contributions to the [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Shrine]] imperial visit preparations. Yoshihira, like his father, served prominent figures such as {{ill|Fujiwara no Michinaga|en|Fujiwara no Michinaga|qid=Q433133|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga|1=Fujiwara no Michinaga}} and {{ill|Fujiwara no Sanesuke|en|Fujiwara no Sanesuke|qid=Q3090659|lt=Fujiwara no Sanesuke|1=Fujiwara no Sanesuke}}. According to the ''[[Hojoju]]'', he performed rituals such as the ''Goryusai'' to invoke rain and the ''Shikaku Shikai Festival'' to ward off calamities. These accounts, supported by sources like the ''{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|en|Nihon Kiryaku|qid=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiryaku|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}'', depict Yoshihira as Abe no Seimei's successor and a central figure in the [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]] traditions, alongside his brother Yoshimasa and [[Kamo clan|Kamo no Mitsuyoshi]]. == Legends == Yoshihira, like his father, became the subject of various legends: * The ''[[Kokin Chomonju]]'' recounts an episode where Yoshihira warned his friend, physician Tanba Masatada, to finish his drink quickly, predicting an imminent earthquake, which indeed occurred shortly thereafter. * In the ''{{ill|Kojidan|en|Kojidan|qid=Q3013995|lt=Kojidan|1=Kojidan}}'', it is noted that the mansion of [[Fujiwara no Morosane]] remained unscathed by fire due to Yoshihira’s protective charms affixed to its structure. These tales illustrate Yoshihira's prowess as an Onmyoji, comparable to his father. His skill in divination earned him the nickname “Sasu no Miko.” == Yoshihira as the Elder Brother Theory == While it is generally believed that Abe no Seimei had two sons—Yoshihira as the eldest and Yoshimasa as the second—some theories, such as those proposed by Shinji Kosaka, suggest that Yoshihira might have been the older but illegitimate brother, while Yoshimasa was the legitimate heir. This speculation arises from the close birth years of Yoshihira and Yoshimasa (exact dates remain unclear). However, references in ''[[Kogiki]]'' by {{ill|Fujiwara no Sanesuke|en|Fujiwara no Sanesuke|qid=Q3090659|lt=Fujiwara no Sanesuke|1=Fujiwara no Sanesuke}} and ''[[Midokanpakuki]]'' by {{ill|Fujiwara no Michinaga|en|Fujiwara no Michinaga|qid=Q433133|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga|1=Fujiwara no Michinaga}} suggest that Yoshihira was more prominent than Yoshimasa, potentially affirming his status as Abe no Seimei’s successor. == Genealogy == * Father: [[Abe no Seimei]] * Mother: Unknown * Spouse: Unknown * Children (mother unknown): ** Son: Abe no Tokichika ** Son: Abe no Hirakazu ** Son: Abe no Kunitoki (later took the monastic name ''Enpitsu'') ** Son: Abe no Akichika ** Son: Abe no Norichika == See also == * [[Abe no Seimei]] * [[:en:Da Liu Ren|Da Liu Ren]] * [[Goryō]] * [[Itako]] * [[Kuji-in]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * {{ill|Sanpaku|en|Sanpaku|qid=Q1815087|lt=Sanpaku|1=Sanpaku}} * [[Seimei Shrine]] * [[Senji Ryakketsu]] * [[Shikigami]] * {{ill|Shinigami|en|Shinigami|qid=Q479033|lt=Shinigami|1=Shinigami}} * [[Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] * [[Ushi no toki mairi]] == Footnotes == === Notes === {{NoteFoot|qq=}} === References === {{Reflist|qq=}} == Further reading == * {{cite book |editor-last=Murakami |editor-first=Shūichi |year=1981 |title=日本陰陽道史総説 |trans-title=A Review of the History of Onmyōdō in Japan |url= |language=ja |location= |publisher=Hanawa Shobō |isbn=4827310572|qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Endō |first=Katsumi |year=1994 |title=近世陰陽道史の研究 |trans-title=Studies in the History of Early Modern Onmyōdō |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher={{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Orai Sha|lt=Shin Jinbutsu Ōrai Sha|ja|新人物往来社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Jinbutsu Orai Sha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Jinbutsu Orai Sha|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11501312}} |isbn=4404021569|qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Kosaka |first=Shinji |year=2004 |title=安倍晴明撰『占事略決』と陰陽道 |trans-title=Abe no Seimei's "Senji Ryakketsu" and Onmyōdō |url=http://www.kyuko.asia/book/b10889.html |language=Japanese |location= |publisher={{ill|Kyuko Shoin|lt=Kyūko Shoin|ja|汲古書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyuko Shoin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyuko Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11552315}} |isbn=9784762941672|qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Saitō |first=Rei |year=2007 |title=王朝時代の陰陽道 |trans-title=Onmyōdō in the Dynastic Period |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Meicho Kankō Kai |isbn=978-4839003302|qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Yamashita |first=Katsuaki |year=1996 |title=平安時代の宗教文化と陰陽道 |trans-title=Religious Culture in the Heian Period and Onmyōdō |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Iwata Shoin |isbn=4900697656|qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Takahashi |first=Keiya |year=2000 |title=現代・陰陽師入門 |trans-title=Introduction to Modern Onmyōji |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=[[Asahi Sonorama]] |isbn=4257035846|qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Nakamura |first=Shōhachi |year=2000 |title=日本陰陽道書の研究 増補版 |trans-title=A Study of the Onmyō Books in Japan; Expanded Edition |url=http://www.kyuko.asia/book/b10888.html |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Kyūko Shoin |isbn=4257035846|qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Suzuki |first=Ikkei |year=2002 |title=陰陽道 呪術と鬼神の世界 |trans-title=Onmyōdō: The World of Spells and Demons |url=https://bookclub.kodansha.co.jp/product?item=0000195204 |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=[[Kodansha|Kōdansha]] |isbn=9784062582445|qq=}} * {{cite book |editor-last=Seimei Shrine |first= |year=2002 |title=安倍晴明公 |trans-title=The Right Honorable Abe no Seimei |url=https://bookclub.kodansha.co.jp/product?item=0000180822 |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Kōdansha |isbn=9784062109833|qq=}} * {{cite book |last1=Hayashi |first1=Jun |last2=Koike |first2=Jun'ichi |year=2002 |title=陰陽道の講義 |trans-title=Lecture on Onmyōdō |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Sagano Shoin |isbn=4782303610|qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Shigeta |first=Shin'ichi |year=2005 |title=平安貴族と陰陽師 |trans-title=Heian Nobles and Onmyōji |url=http://www.yoshikawa-k.co.jp/book/b34635.html |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbun Kan |isbn=4642079424|qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Hayashi |first=Jun |year=2005 |title=近世陰陽道の研究 |trans-title=A Study of Early Modern Onmyōdō |url=http://www.yoshikawa-k.co.jp/book/b32982.html |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbun Kan |isbn=4642034072|qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Shigeta |first=Shin'ichi |year=2006 |title=陰陽師 |trans-title=Onmyōji |url=http://www.chuko.co.jp/ebook/2013/07/514036.html |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=[[Chuokoron-Shinsha|Chūōkōron-Shinsha]] |isbn=4121018443|qq=}} * {{cite book |editor-last=Murakami |editor-first=Shūichi |year=2017 |title=陰陽道叢書 1 古代 |trans-title=Onmyōdō Series 1: The Ancient Times |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Hanawa Shobō |isbn=978-4626017970|qq=}} * {{cite book |editor-last=Murakami |editor-first=Shūichi |year=2017 |title=陰陽道叢書 2 中世 |trans-title=Onmyōdō Series 2: The Middle Ages |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Hanawa Shobō |isbn=978-4626017987|qq=}} * {{cite book |editor-last=Murakami |editor-first=Shūichi |year=2017 |title=陰陽道叢書 3 近世 |trans-title=Onmyōdō Series 3: The Early Modern Period |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Hanawa Shobō |isbn=978-4626017994|qq=}} * {{cite book |editor-last=Murakami |editor-first=Shūichi |year=2017 |title=陰陽道叢書 4 特論 |trans-title=Onmyōdō Series 4: The Advanced Studies |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Hanawa Shobō |isbn=978-4626018007|qq=}}

If I get anywhere near implementing it, I will do so based on the actual completion tiers of this wiki rather than whether I added them when they were on simple english wikipedia or wherever--><!--Abe no Yoshihira--><!--Abe no Yoshihira--><!--Abe no Yoshihira--><!--Abe no Yoshihira--><!--Abe no Yoshihira--><!--Abe no Yoshihira--><!--Abe no Yoshihira--><!--Abe no Yoshihira--><!--Abe no Yoshihira--><!--Abe no Yoshihira--><!--Abe no Yoshihira--><!--Abe no Yoshihira--><!--Abe no Yoshihira--><!--Abe no Yoshihira--><!--Abe no Yoshihira--><!--Abe no Yoshihira--><!--Abe no Yoshihira--><!--Abe no Yoshihira--><!--Abe no Yoshihira--><!--Abe no Yoshihira--><!--Abe no Yoshihira--><!--Abe no Yoshihira-->
